meaning: adv.莉､莠ｺ蝗ｰ諠大慍,菴ｿ莠ｺ霑ｷ荵ｱ蝨ｰ
examples: All the houses looked bewilderingly similar.
The world art market had already been losing momentum for a while after rising bewilderingly since 2003.
As risk has become bewilderingly dispersed, so too has information.
It calls up images of dusty museum collections policed by overseers who are either bewilderingly specialized or blatantly indifferent.
This makes some of the notes in her edition bewilderingly latitudinarian she nods to Zipes, to Bettelheim, to Gilbert and Gubar.
